text:
Book an WIC Appointment | Madera County
What do I need to bring to my first WIC appointment?
WIC staff will see if you are eligible by checking the following:
Identification for you and any children from birth to 5 years old
Proof of current address
For example, phone bill, or water bill
Proof of income
If you have other items as proof of income, address, and ID, please call our WIC office to verify if it will be accepted as proof.
Proof of pregnancy or pregnancy loss
For example, ultrasound picture or a doctor’s note for lost pregnancy within the past 6 months
Trained WIC staff will also conduct a health screening for all family members applying. This includes a sensor placed on the finger (for older infants, children and women), measuring height and weight, and asking you some questions about your health and diet.
At the end of the appointment, you will be told if your family is eligible for WIC services.
To make a WIC appointment, please call us at (559) 675-7623.
What if I miss my appointment?
When you cannot keep or miss your appointment there are a few things that you may do:
The sooner you know you will be unable to keep your appointment, please call so a new appointment can be given. If you miss your appointment, please call as soon as possible to make a new appointment. The best number to call for a WIC appointment is (559) 675-7893.
